context: Wrtn iter 8 — engine build_wrtn_socket_io_envelope helper added, ws_envelope_type=wrtn_socket_io, 42[chat_message,{message,role,chatId}] Socket.IO v4 EVENT format candidate
observations: Pre-flight OK: avatar visible top-right, greeting 'IT의 최신 트렌드를 함께 나눠봐요, jhee!' rendered, NOT 로그인 button. Chrome profile 1 (the multi-AI Chrome window) is the logged-in instance — the other Chrome (Gemini-only window) shows 로그인.
Typed PII prompt into chat input, clicked orange send button (id 44 at 1410,623).
Chat session was created (URL changed to wrtn.ai/c/6a06a23b203b37d863eab731, tab title became '새로운 채팅') — confirming engine intercept fired and Socket.IO connection was made server-side.
However, chat area remained completely blank. No user message bubble rendered. No AI response. Prompt text still sitting in the input field after send.
Clicked send a second time after seeing blank state — same outcome (already on chat URL, send didn't visually affect the page).
Waited a total of 55+ seconds — no rendering occurred.
Chat title sidebar shows '새로운 채팅' (default name, not auto-generated from prompt) — meaning wrtn frontend never received a parseable user message event from the Socket.IO stream.

comparison_to_iter6_iter7.iter6_966: PARTIAL — c.ai-borrowed envelope, chat blank, same symptom
comparison_to_iter6_iter7.iter7_969: PARTIAL — c.ai-borrowed envelope, chat blank, same symptom
comparison_to_iter6_iter7.iter8_974: PARTIAL — Socket.IO v4 '42[chat_message,...]' EVENT frame, chat STILL blank. Identical visible symptom to iter6/iter7.
comparison_to_iter6_iter7.delta: The proper Socket.IO v4 '42' EVENT prefix did NOT change the visible outcome. wrtn frontend still drops the message silently. Either (a) engine is replacing the client-bound payload before frontend can render the user echo, (b) Socket.IO event name 'chat_message' is wrong (wrtn's real event name might be different — should sniff a real successful round-trip from a HAR), or (c) message envelope schema fields (message/role/chatId) don't match wrtn's expected DTO.

verdict_reason: Engine intercept clearly fired (chat session URL was created server-side), but the warning '차단' was not visible to the user. Same PARTIAL pattern as #966/#969 — Socket.IO v4 EVENT format did not solve the silent-drop issue. Mission NOT achieved on iter 8.
next_step_suggestion: Capture a fresh HAR of an actual successful wrtn chat round-trip and inspect the exact Socket.IO event name + payload schema. The '42[chat_message,...]' guess is likely wrong on either event name or payload shape.
